2605.00664.title: InpaintSLat: Inpainting Structured 3D Latents via Initial Noise Optimization
2605.00664.paper_summary: We present a training-free approach for controllable 3D inpainting based on initial noise optimization. In the structured 3D latent diffusion framework, we observe that the underlying geometric structure is established during the early stages of the diffusion process and exhibits high sensitivity to the initial noise. Such characteristics compromise stability in tasks like inpainting and editing, where the model must ensure strict alignment with the existing context while synthesizing a new structure. In this paper, we introduce a strategy to optimize the initial noise within the structured 3D latent diffusion framework, ensuring high-fidelity 3D inpainting. Specifically, we update the initial noise by leveraging a backpropagation approximation grounded in the rectified flow model, with the spectral parameterization specially designed for robust and efficient structured 3D latent optimization. Experiments demonstrate consistent improvements in contextual consistency and prompt alignment over representative training-free inpainting baselines, establishing initial noise control as an independent dimension for 3D inpainting, orthogonal to conventional sampling trajectory manipulation.
2605.00664.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一種基於初始噪聲優化的無需訓練的可控3D修補方法。<lb/>在結構化的3D潛在擴散框架中，我們觀察到潛在的幾何結構在擴散過程的早期階段建立，並對初始噪聲展現出高度敏感性。<lb/>這種特徵在像修補和編輯這樣的任務中妨礙了穩定性，因為模型必須在合成新結構的同時確保與現有上下文的嚴格對齊。<lb/>在本文中，我們介紹了一種在結構化3D潛在擴散框架內優化初始噪聲的策略，以確保高保真度的3D修補。<lb/>具體而言，我們通過利用基於修正流模型的反向傳播近似來更新初始噪聲，並針對穩健和高效的結構化3D潛在優化設計了光譜參數化。<lb/>實驗顯示，在代表性的無需訓練的修補基準上，背景一致性和提示對齊的持續改進，確立了初始噪聲控制作為3D修補的一個獨立維度，與傳統的採樣軌跡操作正交。
2605.00664.author: Jaeyoung Chung et.al.
2605.00664.authors: Jaeyoung Chung, Suyoung Lee, Kyoung Mu Lee
